data.title: United Microelectronics sees 6% revenue growth in February
data.description:
• United Microelectronics (UMC) reported February revenue of NT$19.35M, a 6.33% Y/Y rise.
  • YTD, the Taiwan-based semiconductor company's total revenue reached NT$40.21M, reflecting a 5.81% Y/Y jump.
  • The stock price moved marginally higher to $9.95.

data.title: STMicroelectronics rises as CEO sees data center revenue well above $1B next year
data.description:
STMicroelectronics N.V. (STM) shares advanced in premarket trading on Thursday after the company's CEO, Jean-Marc Chery, projected data center-related revenue to be “well above” $1 billion for next year.

STMicroelectronics (STM) rose 6.6% in Paris trading and 3.3% in early New York trading, extending its year-to-date gain to about 28%.

"So thanks to the first effect of the AWS (AMZN) contract, but amplified by the acceleration of the demand on optical cable, this year, I can say we will be nicely above $1 billion -- $500 million nicely," Chery said during a Morgan Stanley conference on Wednesday.

"And next year, well above $1 billion. So we have really boosting effect from the start of the optical cable and from the benefits of our overall AWS contract that I repeat is a multimillion-dollar contract for the next 5 years that will start this year and moving forward will grow."

For 1Q, Chery said that “the dynamic of booking we receive will enable us to do better than the usual seasonality.”

The chipmaker had said in January that it expected to deliver $1 billion of data center-related revenue before 2030.

data.title: Autohome reports mixed Q4 results; authorizes up to US$200 million share repurchase program
data.description:
• Autohome press release (ATHM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.37 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $209.1M (-17.98% Y/Y) misses by $6.34M.
  • On March 5, 2026, Autohome's Board of Directors authorized a new share repurchase program under which the Company may repurchase up to US$200 million of its American depositary shares over the next eighteen months.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Veeva Systems projects FY outlook above expectations; sees $6B revenue run rate by 2030
Korea’s Lee highlights $68B market stabilization fund after volatility
data.description:
South Korean President Lee Jae Myung on Thursday highlighted the country’s KRW 100T ($68.2B) market stabilization program as authorities worked to contain volatility tied to the escalating conflict in Iran.

The benchmark Kospi (KOSPI) surged as much as 12% on Thursday, its biggest intraday gain since October 2008, after dropping by roughly the same amount the previous day in a turbulent week driven by a global selloff as rising Middle East tensions triggered a flight from risk assets.

KOSPI closed at 5,583.9, up +9.6%. Major companies like Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (HXSC.F) saw gains over 11% and 10%, respectively. Hyundai Motor (HYMLF) +9.4%, Posco Holdings (PKX) +7.7%, LG Energy Solution +6.9%, Naver Corp. +5.7%.

Speaking at a cabinet meeting, Lee instructed officials to manage the market stabilization program “appropriately and swiftly” to prevent instability in financial markets.

“We are not supporting the equity index directly,” Lee said, adding that the fund is intended to prevent “temporary abnormalities, but not to force price adjustments.”

Lee also said the stock market was moving toward normalization. “It’s about properly reforming the economic system and eliminating market unfairness,” he said. “Korea’s stock market being undervalued is a given fact, and it is going through a normalization process.”

The stabilization program was first deployed in 2022 during a debt crisis linked to South Korea’s Legoland development. It includes a credit market stabilization fund to support corporate bonds, along with capital aimed at helping ensure a soft landing for real estate project financing. While the program remains in place during normal periods, authorities can expand it during times of market stress.

Financial Services Commission Chairman Lee Eog-weon said Thursday that the government would increase the size of the fund if necessary.

data.title: Aviva Non-GAAP EPS of 56.00p, revenue of £25.44B
data.description:
• Aviva press release (AVVIY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 56.00p.
  • Revenue of £25.44B (+22.6% Y/Y).

data.title: PepGen GAAP EPS of -$0.27 beats by $0.12
data.description:
• PepGen press release (PEPG): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.27 beats by $0.12.
  • Cash, Cash Equivalents and Marketable Securities were $148.5 million as of December 31, 2025.
  • Based on currently planned operations, the Company believes that its existing cash, cash, equivalents, and marketable securities will be sufficient to fund its operations into the second half of 2027.

data.title: Davide Campari-Milano Non-GAAP EPS of €0.32, revenue of €3.05B; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Davide Campari-Milano press release (DVDCF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €0.32.
  • Revenue of €3.05B (-0.7% Y/Y).
  • Outlook: The pace of underlying organic topline growth is set to continue in 2026, on track reaching mid- to high-single-digit revenue expansion as the Group advances towards its mid-term financial objectives, assuming a challenging yet stable operating environment.
  • Profitability remained robust, supported by contained organic accretion in adjusted EBIT margin, with a second-half weighting due to the front loading of advertising and promotion investments and the base effect of tariff.
  • The gross margin trajectory is expected to remain moderate, with tailwinds offset by an estimated tariffs impact of approximately €30 million based on current levels1

data.title: GM-backed driverless tech firm Momenta said to confidentially file for IPO in Hong Kong
data.description:
• Beijing Momenta Technology Co. is said to have filed confidentially for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, adding to a flurry of share sales in the Asian financial hub.
  • The autonomous driving tech startup, which is backed by General Motors (GM) and Tencent Holdings (TCEHY), is working with China International Capital Corp. (CNICF) and Deutsche Bank (DB) on the listing, Bloomberg News reported. The company has been meeting investors to gauge initial interest in the offering, the report said.

data.title: Trade court orders Trump administration to start refunding IEEPA tariffs
data.description:
A federal trade court judge has ordered the Trump administration to start issuing refunds for the tariffs that were struck down by the Supreme Court last month.

"All importers of record whose entries were subject to IEEPA [International Emergency Economic Powers Act] duties are entitled to the benefit" of the Supreme Court's decision, Judge Richard Eaton at the Court of International Trade said.

The ruling was for a case brought by Atmus Filtration, but Eaton said he will be the only judge who will hear cases pertaining to the refund of IEEPA tariffs.

"So there is no danger that another judge, even one in this court, will reach any contrary conclusions," he noted, and directed the Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to issue refunds after recalculating the duties importers paid and excluding the IEEPA tariffs.

Thousands of lawsuits seeking tariff refunds have been brought by companies including Costco (COST) and FedEx (FDX), many of which were filed before the Supreme Court's ruling.

The Trump administration collected more than $130B in IEEPA tariffs. It could be on the hook for up to $175B in refunds, according to the Penn Wharton Budget Model.

During the hearing for Atmus Filtration's case, the Justice Department's lawyer Claudia Burke said the refund process would be time-consuming as the CBP would have to manually go through millions of import entries.

"We live in the age of computers," Judge Eaton said, as reported by The Wall Street Journal. "It must be possible for Customs Service to program its computers so it doesn't need a manual review."

The Trump administration is expected to appeal the trade court judge's order.
